title: Supported Laterality Coding Correction
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: chart documents right knee sprain, subsequent encounter, with consistent assessment and plan. The submitted ICD code omitted laterality and encounter specificity. No payer outcome, reimbursement impact, or physician action is requested.
expected_high_level_behavior: Identify the supported ICD specificity gap and route for coding correction review without over-escalating the case.

title: Unsupported Specificity Request
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: coding queue asks whether acute systolic heart failure can be selected. The note only states heart failure history and medication refill; no acuity, type, exacerbation, exam findings, or provider assessment support the requested specificity.
expected_high_level_behavior: Avoid assigning unsupported specificity, preserve reviewability, and route for documentation insufficiency or reviewer judgment.

title: Borderline Sepsis Documentation
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: encounter mentions suspected sepsis in the emergency department, fever, and broad-spectrum antibiotics. Later progress note lists localized cellulitis only; discharge summary does not confirm sepsis. Query asks whether sepsis coding is sufficiently supported.
expected_high_level_behavior: Handle the conflicting and borderline evidence conservatively, avoid PASS behavior, and route for specialist or CDI review.

title: Recoverable Missing Lab Linkage
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: anemia code specificity is requested. Provider documents anemia and treatment plan; lab trend is referenced but not included in the packet. No etiology or acuity is documented in the available text.
expected_high_level_behavior: Identify a recoverable documentation gap and route for evidence completion before final coding specificity.

title: Low Evidence Symptom-Only Note
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: intake text says patient returned for follow-up and still feels unwell. No diagnoses, symptom duration, exam findings, treatment rationale, or ICD mapping support are present. Coding support asks for diagnosis specificity.
expected_high_level_behavior: Mark the case as low evidence, avoid unsafe PASS behavior, and route for documentation insufficiency review.

title: Conflicting Laterality Documentation
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: assessment states left shoulder strain, procedure note states right shoulder injection, and coding worksheet proposes right shoulder pain. The available note does not reconcile laterality.
expected_high_level_behavior: Preserve the laterality conflict, avoid selecting a definitive code, and route to coder or CDI review.

title: Conservative Revise for Partial Specificity
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: documentation supports pneumonia but does not identify organism, acuity, or causal linkage. Coding request asks whether a more specific pneumonia code should be used.
expected_high_level_behavior: Keep the supported generic diagnosis visible, avoid unsupported organism specificity, and route for conservative revise review.

title: Low Confidence Coding Support
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: coder proposes chronic kidney disease stage 3b. The note lists CKD, one estimated GFR value near the 3a to 3b boundary, and no trend or provider-stated stage. Query asks whether the proposed specificity is safe before review.
expected_high_level_behavior: Treat the coding support as low confidence, preserve the boundary issue, and route for reviewer judgment before specificity is accepted.

title: Clean Supported Specificity
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: documentation consistently states type 2 diabetes mellitus with mild nonproliferative diabetic retinopathy without macular edema. The assessment, plan, and ophthalmology reference align with the proposed specificity.
expected_high_level_behavior: Allow routine coding-support completion when specificity is clearly supported by consistent documentation.

title: Specialist Review for Stroke Specificity
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: neurology note references possible lacunar infarct, radiology impression says chronic small vessel ischemic change, and discharge diagnosis says dizziness. Coding request asks whether acute cerebral infarction specificity is supported.
expected_high_level_behavior: Preserve specialist-review-worthy ambiguity, avoid unsupported acute stroke specificity, and route conservatively.

title: Over-Cautious Escalation Candidate
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: provider clearly documents acute otitis media, left ear, initial encounter. The coding request asks whether laterality specificity is supported. No conflicting text or missing artifact is present.
expected_high_level_behavior: Avoid unnecessary escalation when documentation supports the requested specificity and route as routine or supported coding review.

title: Coder Review for Procedure Modifier Ambiguity
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: same-day procedure documentation suggests distinct service, but the note does not clearly separate time, site, or clinical rationale. Coding support asks whether modifier use is sufficiently documented.
expected_high_level_behavior: Identify coder-review-worthy modifier ambiguity and avoid definitive modifier support before review.

title: Recoverable Discharge Summary Gap
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: progress notes repeatedly document acute respiratory failure with oxygen support, but the discharge summary problem list omits respiratory failure. Coding request asks whether final coding can proceed from the available packet.
expected_high_level_behavior: Surface the discharge-summary gap as recoverable and route for documentation reconciliation rather than final PASS.

title: Ambiguous Principal Diagnosis Selection
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: admission assessment lists dehydration and acute kidney injury; discharge summary lists weakness and poor intake. Documentation does not clearly identify which condition drove inpatient care. Coding request asks for principal diagnosis support.
expected_high_level_behavior: Preserve principal-diagnosis ambiguity and route for CDI or coder review instead of choosing a principal diagnosis.

title: Insufficient Acuity for Malnutrition
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: nutrition screen mentions weight loss risk and diet counseling. Provider note does not document malnutrition, severity, clinical indicators, or treatment plan. Coding request asks whether severe malnutrition specificity can be used.
expected_high_level_behavior: Reject unsupported severe specificity, identify documentation insufficiency, and keep the case reviewable.

title: Adjacent Case Spillover Check
input_text: Synthetic CDI coding-support note: documentation states uncomplicated urinary tract infection, organism not specified, treated with oral antibiotics. Coding request asks whether organism-specific coding should be added. No systemic infection documentation is present.
expected_high_level_behavior: Keep the supported uncomplicated diagnosis separate from adjacent severe infection concepts and route for conservative coding review if specificity is unsupported.
